reasoning: The requirements for a 'YES' resolution are exceptionally strict: a game must use live-generated AI dialogue (like Nvidia ACE or GPT-4) AND achieve a 90+ on Metacritic or win a major game award. To date in 2025, the flagship titles launching with Nvidia ACE (such as Mecha Break, inZOI, and NARAKA: Bladepoint) have received 'mixed' to 'fair' reviews, with Metacritic scores hovering in the 68–71 range. Furthermore, gaming critics and award panels have shown strong skepticism and even hostility toward generative AI, actively lowering scores or opting not to nominate games heavily utilizing the technology (in part due to industry strikes and artistic concerns). Reviewing the current Wikipedia registry of acclaimed 2025 games (featuring Hades II, Expedition 33, Silksong, etc.), none include live AI dialogue. Major upcoming AAA games like GTA VI will not risk unpredictable live LLM dialogue for their core narratives. Given the technical immaturity, massive PR hurdles, and currently settled 70-tier reviews for the primary test cases, the chance of this resolving YES in 2025 is near zero.
